title: Schedule Drivers Read Surface And Host Validation
objective: Implement the smallest bounded follow-on after the landed 020g-a, 020e.2, and 020h substrate by adding a read-only schedule-drivers surface that exposes critical-path driving edges derived from imported schedule data through the existing GET-only bridge and renders a bounded PM review view for driver chains, without adding schedule writes, drag behavior, client-side fabrication, or transform-builder mutation paths.
dependency_note: UI-002e is no longer blocked by the 020f-era substrate findings. Packet 020e.1 restored loader integrity, 020g-a landed the parser-reconciliation substrate, 020e.2 restored the lost parser-side tests, and 020h admitted a concrete sandbox fixture. Delegate UI-002e against that landed sandbox substrate. If a true host-browser session is not available in this execution context, the packet may close as sandbox-complete with host validation explicitly deferred rather than fabricated.
constrained_outputs: One bounded GET-only schedule bridge extension or query helper exposing critical-path driving-edge records derived from imported schedule relationships and task fields, not the full TASKPRED graph and not a broader float-driver analysis
One minimal PM review surface showing driver task, driven task, relation type, and key dates in a read-only analyst view

Focused test evidence proving the driver surface works end to end from persisted schedule data without adding schedule-write behavior, plus host-browser validation evidence when a real host session is available or an explicit sandbox-complete host-deferred disposition when it is not
validation_steps: Confirm the schedule bridge remains GET-only and read-only by construction
Confirm the first slice exposes critical-path driving edges only, not the full dependency graph and not float-driver analysis, and does not fabricate dependency semantics in the client
Confirm no drag-to-reschedule, inline dependency editing, schedule writes, or transform-builder behavior is introduced

Confirm host-side browser validation succeeds against the local mutation seam and PM surface when a real host session is available; otherwise close truthfully as sandbox-complete with host validation deferred
sql_permission_note: Packet UI-002e authorizes only bounded GET-only bridge/query and read-only PM UI work over existing persisted schedule data. No new SQL, no schedule-write behavior, and no client-side fabrication are authorized.
